title: Sticky Glazed Chicken
tagline: One seared chicken base, two glazes — honey-garlic or bright orange. Great in a bowl or a wrap.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients using chicken breast and the honey-garlic glaze.
Excellent gym macros — ~45 g protein per serving. Chicken thigh is juicier but adds ~120 kcal per serving in fat.
The glaze sugar (honey) is the main calorie lever: drop honey to 3 tbsp and skip the finishing butter to save ~80 kcal per serving.
Soya doesn't suit this seared-chicken dish; protein is already high without it.

items: 400 g boneless chicken — breast (leaner) or thigh (juicier), cut into bite-size pieces
1 tbsp cornflour (cornstarch) — for light crispness and to help the glaze cling
1/2 tsp salt (skip if your soy is very salty)
1 tbsp rice or apple cider vinegar (balances the sweetness — don't skip)
1/2 cup (120 ml) fresh orange juice — ~2 kinnows or 2-3 Nagpur santras
1-2 tsp honey (taste first — Indian oranges are sweet)
1/2 tsp cornflour + 1 tbsp water (slurry, to thicken)

steps.title: Marinate the chicken
steps.body: Toss the 400 g chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp cornflour, 1/2 tsp pepper and 1/2 tsp salt. Rest 10 minutes (even 5 helps) — the cornflour gives a light crust that grabs the glaze.

steps.title: Sear (or air fry)
steps.body: Heat a wide pan on medium-high with 1.5 tbsp oil. Add the chicken in a single layer (don't crowd) and cook 2-3 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Remove and set aside. For extra crisp, air fry at 200°C for 10-12 min instead, then glaze at the end.

steps.title: Build your glaze
steps.body: Lower heat to medium. For HONEY-GARLIC: sauté the minced garlic (+ 1/2 tsp chilli flakes) 15-20 seconds without browning, then add 4 tbsp honey, 2 tbsp soy, 1 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp ketchup/oyster sauce and 1/4 cup water; simmer 1-2 min until slightly thick. For ORANGE: sauté the garlic 20 sec, add 1/2 cup orange juice, 1 tsp zest, 1 tbsp soy and 1-2 tsp honey; simmer 3-4 min, then stir in the cornflour slurry and reduce until it coats a spoon.

steps.title: Glaze the chicken
steps.body: Return the chicken to the pan and toss continuously on medium heat 1-2 minutes until the sauce turns glossy and coats every piece. Swirl in 1 tsp butter at the end if using. If too thick, add 1-2 tbsp water; too thin, simmer 30-40 sec more — you want sticky, not syrupy or watery.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Kill the heat, scatter over spring onions and sesame seeds. Serve over rice, in a bowl with stir-fried veg, or wrap it up.

notes: Wrap version: warm a tortilla/roti, spread a thin strip of light mayo (1.5 tbsp mayo + 1/2 tsp lemon), lay lettuce or cabbage FIRST as a moisture barrier, then the glazed chicken (don't pour extra sauce — honey glazes leak), optional onion/cheese, and roll tight. Toast seam-side down 30-40 sec to seal.
Kinnow oranges are the best for the orange glaze; with sweet mausambi add a squeeze of lemon for brightness, and with Nagpur santra cut the honey.

Defrost note: microwave-defrosted chicken can throw a brief warm/fatty smell and pool some fat — that's usually fine. Tip out the pooled liquid, rinse once, pat very dry, and cook straight away on high heat. If the smell is sour, sulphury or ammonia-like and persists after rinsing, throw it out — cooking won't fix spoiled chicken.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
